back_translation: These are the journeys of the sons of Israel, who came out from the land of Egypt by their divisions, by the hand of Moses and Aaron.
And Moses wrote their departures by their journeys, by the mouth of the LORD; and these are their journeys by their departures.
And they journeyed from Rameses in the first month, on the fifteenth day of the first month; on the day after the Passover, the sons of Israel went out with a high hand, in the eyes of all Egypt.
And the Egyptians were burying — what the LORD had struck among them — every firstborn; and on their gods the LORD executed judgments.
And the sons of Israel journeyed from Rameses, and camped in Succoth.
And they journeyed from Succoth, and camped in Etham, which is at the edge of the wilderness.
And they journeyed from Etham, and turned back to Pi-hahiroth, which faces Baal-zephon; and they camped before Migdol.
And they journeyed from before Hahiroth, and crossed through the midst of the sea into the wilderness, and went a journey of three days in the wilderness of Etham, and camped at Marah.
And they journeyed from Marah, and came to Elim; and in Elim were twelve springs of water and seventy palm trees, and they camped there.
And they journeyed from Elim, and camped by the Red Sea.
And they journeyed from the Red Sea, and camped in the Desert of Sin.
And they journeyed from the Desert of Sin, and camped at Dophkah.
And they journeyed from Dophkah, and camped at Alush.
And they journeyed from Alush, and camped at Rephidim, and there was no water there for the people to drink.
And they journeyed from Rephidim, and camped in the Wilderness of Sinai.
And they journeyed from the Wilderness of Sinai, and camped at Kibroth-hattaavah.
And they journeyed from Kibroth-hattaavah, and camped at Hazeroth.
And they journeyed from Hazeroth, and camped at Rithmah.
And they journeyed from Rithmah, and camped at Rimmon-perez.
And they journeyed from Rimmon-perez, and camped at Libnah.
And they journeyed from Libnah, and camped at Rissah.
And they journeyed from Rissah, and camped at Kehelathah.
And they journeyed from Kehelathah, and camped at Mount Shepher.
And they journeyed from Mount Shepher, and camped at Haradah.
And they journeyed from Haradah, and camped at Makheloth.
And they journeyed from Makheloth, and camped at Tahath.
And they journeyed from Tahath, and camped at Terah.
And they journeyed from Terah, and camped at Mithkah.
And they journeyed from Mithkah, and camped at Hashmonah.
And they journeyed from Hashmonah, and camped at Moseroth.
And they journeyed from Moseroth, and camped at Bene-jaakan.
And they journeyed from Bene-jaakan, and camped at Hor-haggidgad.
And they journeyed from Hor-haggidgad, and camped at Jotbathah.
And they journeyed from Jotbathah, and camped at Abronah.
And they journeyed from Abronah, and camped at Ezion-geber.
And they journeyed from Ezion-geber, and camped in the Wilderness of Zin, that is Kadesh.
And they journeyed from Kadesh, and camped at Mount Hor, at the edge of the land of Edom.

And Aaron the priest went up to Mount Hor by the mouth of the LORD, and died there, in the fortieth year of the coming out of the sons of Israel from the land of Egypt, in the fifth month, on the first of the month.
And Aaron was a son of 123 years at his death on Mount Hor.
And the Canaanite, king of Arad — and he was dwelling in the Negev in the land of Canaan — heard of the coming of the sons of Israel.
And they journeyed from Mount Hor, and camped at Zalmonah.
And they journeyed from Zalmonah, and camped at Punon.
And they journeyed from Punon, and camped at Oboth.
And they journeyed from Oboth, and camped at Iye-abarim on the border of Moab.
And they journeyed from Iyim, and camped at Dibon-gad.
And they journeyed from Dibon-gad, and camped at Almon-diblathaim.
And they journeyed from Almon-diblathaim, and camped in the mountains of Abarim before Nebo.
And they journeyed from the mountains of Abarim, and camped in the plains of Moab by the Jordan of Jericho.
And they camped by the Jordan from Beth-jeshimoth as far as Abel-shittim, in the plains of Moab.
And the LORD spoke to Moses in the plains of Moab by the Jordan of Jericho, saying:
'Speak to the sons of Israel, and say to them: When you cross the Jordan to the land of Canaan.'

'And you shall dispossess all the inhabitants of the land from before you, and you shall destroy all their figured stones, and all their cast images you shall destroy, and all their high places you shall demolish.'
'And you shall dispossess the land and dwell in it, for I have given to you the land to possess it.'

'And you shall inherit the land by lot according to your clans; to the many you shall increase his inheritance, and to the few you shall decrease his inheritance; wherever the lot falls to him, there it shall be his; according to the tribes of your fathers you shall inherit.'

'But if you do not dispossess the inhabitants of the land from before you, then it shall be — those whom you let remain of them shall be barbs in your eyes and thorns in your sides, and they shall harass you on the land in which you dwell.'
'And it shall be — as I planned to do to them, so I will do to you.'
